message:
fix(indexer): optimize lexical rebuild query ordering and status reporting

Three interrelated improvements to the indexer and status subsystems:

1. Lexical rebuild query optimization (storage/sqlite.rs, indexer/mod.rs):
   Add list_conversations_for_lexical_rebuild() which orders by primary
   key (c.id ASC) instead of the user-facing recency sort. The recency
   sort forces SQLite to build a temporary B-tree on every page during
   full rebuilds, which is unnecessary since rebuilds only need stable
   traversal order. The indexer now calls this optimized method.

2. Rebuild status accuracy (lib.rs):
   The rebuild_active flag now uses the index-run lock directly instead
   of requiring both the lock AND a lexical snapshot. The previous logic
   would report rebuild_active=false during the early phases of a rebuild
   (before the first lexical checkpoint is written), causing `cass status`
   to misleadingly show no active rebuild. Also fall back to the lock's
   started_at_ms when no snapshot timestamp exists yet.

3. Daily stats rebuild robustness (storage/sqlite.rs):
   - Increase default batch sizes (400→1000 conversations, add 10k
     message batch size) with env-var overrides
   - Add OOM resilience: automatically halve batch size on out-of-memory
     errors and retry
   - Use per-batch StatsAggregator instead of accumulating across the
     entire rebuild to reduce peak memory
   - Detect and prefer message_metrics table over raw messages table
     when coverage is complete
   - Wrap rebuild in a single transaction for atomicity

Includes a new test verifying that cass status correctly reports an
active rebuild before any lexical snapshot exists.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
